summary: Made the M2 video path HDR/color evidence boundary explicit and machine-checkable. The bundle now records that the existing sample is yuv420p with tv range and unknown color tags, and that it does not validate HDR handling, color-management behavior, wide-gamut preservation, or end-to-end color accuracy.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: The HDR/color boundary is explicit and machine-checkable.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: bundle-manifest.json includes evidenceBoundary.sampleColorMetadata and false machineCheckableClaims for HDR handling, color-management behavior, wide-gamut preservation, and end-to-end color accuracy.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: The existing sample's color metadata is recorded.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: sampleColorMetadata records pixelFormat=yuv420p, colorRange=tv, and unknown colorSpace, colorTransfer, and colorPrimaries.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Downstream users are warned not to treat the sample as HDR/color proof.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: bundle-manifest.json, evidence/m2-video-path-validation/notes.md, and evidence/m2-nvenc-encode-sample/notes.md all state that HDR/color behavior is not validated.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Future HDR/color validation is noted as future work, not completed evidence.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: bundle-manifest.json evidenceBoundary.futureWorkForHdrColorValidation and notes.md identify the required future proof and state it is not present.
commands.log, report.md, json-validation.log, and completion.json are present for this node.

commandsRun.evidence: No file exceeded the repository line-count limit.

realWorldValidation.evidence: This node only clarifies evidence boundaries and non-claims. It does not implement or validate HDR or color-management support.
